item.title: Shopify Drops as Forecast Suggests Slowing Revenue Growth Pace
item.description: Shopify Inc. shares slumped as its revenue outlook suggests growth pace may be slowing down.

item.title: Gilts Selloff Pushes 30-Year Yield to the Highest Since 1998
item.description: The yield on long-dated UK debt climbed to a 28-year high as traders bet on more Bank of England interest-rate hikes.

item.title: RBA Leads World With Third Hike as Iran War Adds to Inflation Pressure
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia delivered a third straight interest-rate hike, unwinding all of last year’s reductions.

item.title: Erste Plans SRT Tied to Austrian Commercial Real Estate Loans
item.description: Erste Group Bank AG is working on a significant risk transfer tied to commercial real estate loans in Austria as regulators push lenders to bolster their capital buffers against potential defaults in the sector.

item.title: Ferrari Profit Beats Views as Higher Prices Outweigh Volume Drop
item.description: Ferrari NV reported a rise in first-quarter earnings that beat analysts’ estimates, as personal add-ons and a richer mix of supercars outweighed a wartime drop in deliveries.

item.title: EBay Investor Skeptical That GameStop Can Make Good on Acquisition Offer
item.description: Bill Smead says he’s not sure where GameStop will find the money

item.title: Amazon Opens Its Logistics Network to Broader Freight Market
item.description: The e-commerce giant is bringing its reputation for speed and reliability to the broader logistics industry.

item.title: Harbour’s Tie-Up With Waldorf Cleared in Court Defeat for HMRC
item.description: Harbour Energy Plc is set to acquire Waldorf Production UK after a London court dismissed an objection to plans to write off most of Waldorf’s unpaid taxes.

item.title: Crypto Exchange Bullish to Buy Equiniti In $4.2 Billion Deal
item.description: Bullish agreed to buy Equiniti from Siris Capital in a $4.2 billion deal, as the crypto exchange seeks to expand in blockchain-based capital markets infrastructure.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Sees More Stock Gains After Narrow Rally
item.description: The rebound in global stocks from their Iran war lows has been so narrow that the market is primed for broader gains triggered by even slightly positive news, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: US Companies Are Eagerly Buying Back Their Own Shares
item.description: Companies have announced $665 billion of buybacks this year.

item.title: Prysmian CEO Targets €4 Billion M&A as Hyperscaler Deals Near
item.description: Italian cable maker Prysmian SpA is scouting the market for a potential acquisition of about $4 billion enterprise value, Chief Executive Officer Massimo Battaini said in an interview with Bloomberg TV on Tuesday.

item.title: Philippines to Deepen Japan Ties While US Focuses on Iran
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. told visiting Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi that their countries, both US treaty allies, will need to deepen cooperation in Asia even as Washington focuses much of its attention on the Middle East.

item.title: AMD Earnings Need to Justify Rally After Best Month in 25 Years
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is coming off its best month in the stock market since the dot-com era, and with the chipmaker’s first-quarter earnings due after the bell, investors will get a chance to see whether its shares have gotten ahead of its fundamentals.

item.title: ADM Raises Profit Outlook as US Biofuels Policy Boosts Demand
item.description: Archer-Daniels-Midland Co. increased its annual earnings outlook as the crop trader expects a clearer US biofuels policy to offset market disruptions from the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: UK to Introduce 12-Month Treasury Bills to Broaden Debt Market
item.description: The UK will begin selling 12-month Treasury bills at its weekly auctions, as part of the government’s efforts to tilt its borrowing toward shorter-maturity debt.

item.title: Brazil Nears Its China Beef Quota in Big Shift for Trade Flows
item.description: The world’s top beef exporter Brazil is close to reaching its annual quota for shipments to its biggest buyer China, a situation that’s set to redraw trade flows at a time when global consumers are facing higher meat prices.

item.title: IronSource Founders’ AI Startup Zyg Raises at $500 Million Value
item.description: Zyg, an artificial intelligence platform created by the founders of IronSource, raised funding at a $500 million valuation just two months after coming out of stealth.

item.title: RBI Mulls Foreign Bond Issuance by State Banks After Rupee Slump
item.description: India’s central bank, grappling to support the beleaguered rupee, is weighing a plan for state-owned lenders to sell foreign-currency bonds to draw capital inflows, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ratcliffe’s Ineos Markets New Bond After Boost From Iran War
item.description: Ineos Group, the heavily-indebted chemicals conglomerate helmed by Jim Ratcliffe, plans to raise €400 million ($468 million) from bonds to repay upcoming term loans.

item.title: Emerging Assets Drop as Middle East Flareup Weighs on Sentiment
item.description: The currency and stock benchmarks for developing economies declined as a flareup in the Middle East conflict reinforced concerns over a global inflation spike and curbed risk appetite.

item.title: Singapore’s Marina One Office Complex Said to Attract CapitaLand, Hongkong Land
item.description: CapitaLand Group Pte and Hongkong Land Holdings Ltd. are among possible bidders for the Marina One high-rise complex in Singapore’s central business district, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Sadler Accused Bankers of Botching Block Trade as He Sold Stock
item.description: Segantii Capital Management founder Simon Sadler accused bankers of mishandling a block trade in fashion retailer Esprit Holdings Ltd. as his own firm was shorting the stock, a Hong Kong court heard on Tuesday.

item.title: Hong Kong Stays Hot for Medical Equipment Maker Debut: ECM Watch
item.description: Shares of Star Sports Medicine Co. in their Hong Kong trading debut on Tuesday, extending a run of strong listings in the city in recent months as investors showed that appetite for Chinese stocks extends beyond companies linked to artificial intellgence.

item.title: Drowning in Debt, Eight Million Firms Miss Payments in Brazil
item.description: Take a glance at the stock market, and all appears well in the Brazilian economy. The Ibovespa has soared more than any other major index in the Americas over the past year — almost 60% in dollar terms at last count.

item.title: BNP Banker Fired Over Bullying Allegations Loses His $2 Million Payout
item.description: A senior BNP Paribas SA banker who was fired for allegedly bullying his subordinates on the trading floor lost a €1.7 million ($2 million) unfair-dismissal award on appeal after judges drew on testimony from staffers.

item.title: Chrysalis Writes Down Stake in UK Digital Bank Starling by 14%
item.description: Chrysalis Investments Ltd. cut the value of its stake in UK financial technology firm Starling Bank, as the conflict in the Middle East impacted stock markets and the digital bank’s peers.

item.title: Israel-Focused Energean Looks to Africa in Diversification Push
item.description: Energean Plc is planning to scale up operations in Africa to diversify from its focus on the Middle East as conflict in the region roils energy markets.

item.title: Nscale Plans Expansion of Microsoft Data Center Site in Portugal
item.description: Nscale Global Holdings has agreed to spend €695 million ($812 million) on infrastructure in Portugal in an expansion of its partnership with Microsoft Corp.

item.title: Bridge Growth Raises $790 Million to Extend Hold on Solace
item.description: Bridge Growth Partners has raised $790 million of equity in a secondary transaction that will let it hold the infrastructure software company Solace for longer.
